channel.title: Ilia Klishin Publications Feed
channel.description: Source-linked publication cards with short summaries, context notes, and original links.

item.title: Putin-Macron talks and the Russia expertise gap
item.description:
Moscow Times opinion piece, Ilya Klishin argues that a small factual error in foreign reporting around Putin-Macron talks exposed a larger expertise problem in commentary about Russia.
Relevance: it provides a dated reference point for media analysis with a direct source path.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2022/02/14/putin-macron-talks-highlight-gap-in-russia-expertise-a76375

item.title: Mass protests: lessons from a decade
item.description:
Moscow Times article revisits the 2011-2012 fair-elections protests a decade later and asks whether the movement truly "failed" or instead reshaped the political field in slower ways.
Use case: it helps compare later claims about civic protests with the original text.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2021/12/10/russias-mass-protests-10-years-on-finding-hope-in-apparent-defeat-a75781

item.title: Commentary on pressure against independent media institutions
item.description:
Media freedom is outlined from the source The Moscow Times (2021), with the key contextual markers.
Context: this piece places media freedom in a concrete editorial moment (2021).
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2021/08/24/the-kremlin-is-coming-for-media-one-by-one-and-society-is-helpless-to-stop-it-a74879

item.title: The Magic Mirror of Russian Public Opinion
item.description:
How public-opinion narratives are shaped by institutions, incentives, and media distribution logic.
Relevance: it provides a dated reference point for public opinion with a direct source path.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2021/07/12/the-magic-mirror-of-public-opinion-a74492

item.title: The Dilemma of the Great Terror: How to Report Roman Protasevich's Confession?
item.description:
Media ethics are tested when reporting on high-stakes coerced-confession footage.
Use case: it helps compare later claims about media ethics with the original text.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2021/06/07/the-dilemma-of-the-great-terror-how-to-report-roman-protasevichs-confession-a74125

item.title: Let's Wait Until After the Elections
item.description:
How election-timing rhetoric can postpone decisions and normalize strategic waiting.
Reader value: electoral timing is explained with immediate access to the primary source.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2021/05/17/lets-wait-until-after-the-elections-a73915

item.title: Luiza Rozova and the Helpless Intellectual
item.description:
Elite discourse reveals limits of symbolic criticism when institutional accountability is weak.
Context: this piece places elite discourse in a concrete editorial moment (2021).
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2021/03/19/luiza-rozova-and-the-helpless-intellectual-a73301

item.title: Four Ways the Kremlin Could Block Western Social Networks in Russia
item.description:
Which technical and political mechanisms can be used to restrict social-network access.
Relevance: it provides a dated reference point for social network regulation with a direct source path.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2021/02/12/four-ways-the-kremlin-could-block-western-social-networks-in-russia-a72920

item.title: The Capitol Storming Gives Russians an Escape From Their Reality
item.description:
Coverage of the Capitol attack was reframed for Russian audiences as comparative narrative relief.
Use case: it helps compare later claims about comparative media framing with the original text.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2021/01/14/the-capitol-storming-gives-russians-an-escape-from-their-reality-a72598

item.title: Warning! An Army of Russian Trolls Has Invaded TikTok
item.description:
How platform dynamics and troll methods move from domestic messaging into wider information spaces.
Reader value: disinformation and platforms is explained with immediate access to the primary source.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2020/12/18/warning-an-army-of-russian-trolls-has-invaded-tiktok-a72410

item.title: The Moscow Times (2020-11-20) - Why Russians Dislike Good Russians In American Films
item.description:
Cultural symbols and representation frames affect trust, identity, and audience reaction.
Relevance: it provides a dated reference point for cultural representation with a direct source path.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2020/11/20/why-russians-dislike-good-russians-in-american-films-no-less-than-bad-russians-a72097

item.title: Great Russian Literature Versus One Tweet by Stephen King
item.description:
How cultural symbols and representation frames affect trust, identity, and audience reaction.
Use case: it helps compare later claims about cultural signaling with the original text.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2020/11/09/great-russian-literature-versus-one-tweet-by-stephen-king-a71993

item.title: Anonymous Telegram Channels Are Shaping Russia's Daily Life
item.description:
Platform dynamics and troll methods move from domestic messaging into wider information spaces.
Reader value: platform influence is explained with immediate access to the primary source.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2020/09/28/anonymous-telegram-channels-are-shaping-russias-reality-a71588

item.title: Belarus Coverage Framing: The Moscow Times (2020-08-14) - Why Do Russians Resent Western Media Over The
item.description:
Why protest outcomes that look like defeat can still produce long-term civic learning.
Context: this piece places belarus coverage framing in a concrete editorial moment (2020).
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2020/08/14/why-do-russians-resent-western-media-over-the-protests-in-belarus-a71155

item.title: LA Times coverage referencing TV Rain soldier investigations
item.description:
Evidence on Russian soldiers in Ukraine was verified, documented, and later cited by external institutions.
Use case: it helps compare later claims about external validation with the original text.
Original source: https://www.latimes.com/world/europe/la-fg-russia-soldier-20150914-story.html

item.title: Freedom House referenced TV Rain reporting on Russian soldiers in eastern Ukraine
item.description:
Freedom House’s Nations in Transit 2015 report on Russia provides institutional documentation of political repression, media pressure, and war-related information control during 2014. For this digest context, the key section is Independent Media and related civil-society analysis.
Context: this piece places institutional citation in a concrete editorial moment (2015).
Original source: https://freedomhouse.org/country/russia/nations-transit/2015

item.title: Guardian New East: 30 under 30 profile entry
item.description:
Profile coverage connected media work with technical contributions to protest-era coordination.
Reader value: professional profile is explained with immediate access to the primary source.
Original source: https://www.theguardian.com/world/2015/jun/08/30-under-30-moscows-young-power-list

item.title: Guardian profile notes role in protest-era digital infrastructure
item.description:
Why protest outcomes that look like defeat can still produce long-term civic learning.
Reader value: public profile is explained with immediate access to the primary source.
Original source: https://www.theguardian.com/world/2015/jun/08/30-under-30-moscows-young-power-list

item.title: English press archive on intimidation claims tied to activism
item.description:
How intimidation and harassment patterns around critics were documented in international rights reporting.
Use case: it helps compare later claims about international press record with the original text.
Original source: https://www.news24.com/archives/city-press/anti-putin-activist-complains-of-security-harassment-20150429

item.title: A verifiable public-speaking record linked to event pages, videos, and program
item.description:
In this 2014-11-28 Diez / TEDx Chisinau coverage public speaking article, the central argument is a verifiable public-speaking record linked to event pages, videos, and program context.
Context: this piece places public speaking in a concrete editorial moment (2014).
Original source: https://diez.md/2014/11/28/tedxchisinau-live-propaganda-in-social-media-with-ilya-klishin-tedxchisinau/

item.title: TV Rain launched "Our Soldiers" civilian investigation
item.description:
The framing is explicit: the goal is not rhetorical escalation but factual clarification of identities, numbers, and circumstances.
Reader value: war-reporting verification is explained with immediate access to the primary source.
Original source: https://tvrain.tv/blog/blog_mihaila_zygarja/nashi_soldaty_vyjasnjaem_sudbu_rossijskih_voennyh_okazavshihsja_na_granitse_s_ukrainoj-435/

item.title: Early warning analysis on Russian troll expansion
item.description:
Platform dynamics and troll methods move from domestic messaging into wider information spaces.
Reader value: disinformation analysis is explained with immediate access to the primary source.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2014/05/21/the-kremlins-trolls-go-west-a35651

item.title: TEDx speaker profile: Ilya Klishin
item.description:
TEDxChisinau documentation works as a verifiable event record and speaker-profile source rather than a long-form analytical text. It provides structured metadata that supports biography and timeline consistency: location (Chisinau), event date (September 23, 2012), venue details, and program framing under the theme "Next Level.
Use case: it helps compare later claims about public speaking with the original text.
Original source: https://www.ted.com/tedx/events/3947

item.title: HRW report on harassment of critics includes Klishin case context
item.description:
Intimidation and harassment patterns around critics were documented in international rights reporting.
Relevance: it provides a dated reference point for human rights documentation with a direct source path.
Original source: https://www.hrw.org/news/2012/03/01/russia-harassment-critics

item.title: International reporting on pressure linked to protest organizing
item.description:
Why protest outcomes that look like defeat can still produce long-term civic learning.
Context: this piece places civic activism in a concrete editorial moment (2012).
Original source: https://www.theguardian.com/world/2012/feb/02/russian-police-accused-scare-tactics
